title: Contact lens red eye documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.
red_flags.rationale: Supports clinician-assessed eye red flags for contact-lens red eye; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

red_flags.text: Identifies microbial keratitis as sight-threatening and supports urgent ophthalmology documentation; treatment specifics are not mapped.
red_flags.rationale: Supports sight-threatening keratitis emergency context for contact-lens red eye; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Identifies microbial keratitis as sight-threatening and supports urgent ophthalmology documentation; treatment specifics are not mapped.

source.title: Clinical Management Guideline: Microbial keratitis (bacterial, fungal)

exact_section.heading: Patient summary — sight-threatening emergency and referral

follow_up.text: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.
follow_up.rationale: Supports contact-lens history and conjunctivitis diagnostic pitfalls for contact-lens red eye; no decision is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

exact_section.heading: Section 7 — diagnosis, history, pitfalls, and contact-lens wearers

safety_netting.text: Supports contact-lens use, usually unilateral redness, watering, foreign-body sensation, discomfort, photophobia, peripheral corneal opacity, and the need to distinguish sight-threatening microbial keratitis.
safety_netting.rationale: Supports contact-lens-associated infiltrative keratitis presentation for contact-lens red eye; no decision is generated.
safety_netting.evidence_extract: Supports contact-lens use, usually unilateral redness, watering, foreign-body sensation, discomfort, photophobia, peripheral corneal opacity, and the need to distinguish sight-threatening microbial keratitis.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Lens, wear and hygiene, eye, onset, pain, photophobia, discharge, vision, corneal findings, examination and clinician disposition are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for contact-lens red eye.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
